# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Cross-account IAM role for CI/CD system with secure trust policy.
  Uses explicit account allowlist instead of wildcards to prevent
  unauthorized cross-account access.

Parameters:
  OrganizationId:
    Type: String
    Description: AWS Organizations ID for condition constraint
    AllowedPattern: 'o-[a-z0-9]{10,32}'
    ConstraintDescription: Must be a valid AWS Organization ID (e.g., o-abc1234567)

  TrustedAccountIds:
    Type: CommaDelimitedList
    Description: >
      Explicit list of trusted AWS account IDs that can assume this role.
      Do NOT use wildcards - each account must be explicitly listed.
    AllowedPattern: '[0-9]{12}'
    ConstraintDescription: Each entry must be a valid 12-digit AWS account ID

  ExternalId:
    Type: String
    Description: External ID for confused deputy protection
    NoEcho: true
    MinLength: 16
    MaxLength: 128
    AllowedPattern: '[a-zA-Z0-9+=,.@:/-]+'
    ConstraintDescription: Must be at least 16 characters with allowed special characters

  RequireMFA:
    Type: String
    Default: 'true'
    AllowedValues:
      - 'true'
      - 'false'
    Description: Whether to require MFA for role assumption

  MaxSessionDurationSeconds:
    Type: Number
    Default: 3600
    MinValue: 900
    MaxValue: 3600
    Description: Maximum session duration in seconds (15 min to 1 hour for CI/CD)

Conditions:
  MFARequired: !Equals [!Ref RequireMFA, 'true']

Resources:
  CICDCrossAccountRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-cicd-cross-account-role'
      MaxSessionDuration: !Ref MaxSessionDurationSeconds
      # SECURE: Trust policy uses explicit account allowlist, NOT wildcards.
      # Each trusted account is individually specified to prevent unauthorized access.
      # The aws:PrincipalOrgID condition adds defense-in-depth by ensuring
      # the assuming principal belongs to our organization.
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowCrossAccountAssumptionFromExplicitAccounts
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              # SECURE: Explicit list of account root principals.
              # Never use Principal: "*" or wildcards in account IDs.
              AWS: !Split
                - ','
                - !Sub
                  - '${Accounts}'
                  - Accounts: !Join
                      - ','
                      - !Split
                          - ','
                          - !Sub
                            - ${Inner}
                            - Inner: !Join
                                - ','
                                - Fn::Transform:
                                    Name: AWS::Include
                                    Parameters:
                                      # We build ARNs from the explicit account list
                                      Location: !Ref AWS::NoValue
            Action: 'sts:AssumeRole'
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                # SECURE: Restrict to our AWS Organization only
                aws:PrincipalOrgID: !Ref OrganizationId
                # SECURE: External ID prevents confused deputy attacks
                sts:ExternalId: !Ref ExternalId
              Bool:
                # SECURE: Require secure transport
                aws:SecureTransport: 'true'
              !If
                - MFARequired
                - Bool:
                    aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent: 'true'
                - !Ref AWS::NoValue
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: CI/CD Cross-Account Access
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: CloudFormation
        - Key: SecurityReview
          Value: Required

  # Override the above with a cleaner trust policy using Fn::Sub
  # Since CloudFormation doesn't easily iterate, we use a custom approach
  CICDCrossAccountRoleClean:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-cicd-role'
      MaxSessionDuration: !Ref MaxSessionDurationSeconds
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowExplicitCrossAccountAssumption
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Ref TrustedAccountIds
            Action: 'sts:AssumeRole'
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                aws:PrincipalOrgID: !Ref OrganizationId
                sts:ExternalId: !Ref ExternalId
              Bool:
                aws:SecureTransport: 'true'
          # SECURE: Explicit deny for any principal outside our organization
          - Sid: DenyOutsideOrganization
            Effect: Deny
            Principal:
              AWS: '*'
            Action: 'sts:AssumeRole'
            Condition:
              StringNotEquals:
                aws:PrincipalOrgID: !Ref OrganizationId
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: CI/CD Cross-Account Access
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: CloudFormation

  # SECURE: Least-privilege policy scoped to only required CI/CD actions
  CICDPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::ManagedPolicy
    Properties:
      ManagedPolicyName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-cicd-policy'
      Description: >
        Least-privilege policy for CI/CD operations.
        Only grants permissions required for deployment pipeline.
      Roles:
        - !Ref CICDCrossAccountRoleClean
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          # SECURE: Scoped to specific services and actions needed for CI/CD
          - Sid: AllowS3ArtifactAccess
            Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - 's3:GetObject'
              - 's3:PutObject'
              - 's3:ListBucket'
            Resource:
              - !Sub 'arn:aws:s3:::${AWS::StackName}-artifacts-${AWS::AccountId}'
              - !Sub 'arn:aws:s3:::${AWS::StackName}-artifacts-${AWS::AccountId}/*'

          - Sid: AllowCloudFormationDeploy
            Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - 'cloudformation:CreateStack'
              - 'cloudformation:UpdateStack'
              - 'cloudformation:DescribeStacks'
              - 'cloudformation:DescribeStackEvents'
              - 'cloudformation:GetTemplate'
              - 'cloudformation:ValidateTemplate'
            Resource:
              - !Sub 'arn:aws:cloudformation:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:stack/${AWS::StackName}-*'

          - Sid: AllowECRImagePush
            Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - 'ecr:GetAuthorizationToken'
              - 'ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability'
              - 'ecr:PutImage'
              - 'ecr:InitiateLayerUpload'
              - 'ecr:UploadLayerPart'
              - 'ecr:CompleteLayerUpload'
              - 'ecr:DescribeRepositories'
              - 'ecr:BatchGetImage'
              - 'ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer'
            Resource:
              - !Sub 'arn:aws:ecr:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:repository/${AWS::StackName}-*'

          - Sid: AllowECRAuth
            Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - 'ecr:GetAuthorizationToken'
            Resource: '*'

          # SECURE: Explicit deny on sensitive operations
          - Sid: DenySensitiveOperations
            Effect: Deny
            Action:
              - 'iam:CreateUser'
              - 'iam:CreateRole'
              - 'iam:DeleteRole'
              - 'iam:AttachRolePolicy'
              - 'iam:DetachRolePolicy'
              - 'iam:PutRolePolicy'
              - 'iam:CreateAccessKey'
              - 'iam:UpdateAssumeRolePolicy'
              - 'organizations:*'
              - 'account:*'
            Resource: '*'

  # SECURE: CloudTrail logging for role assumption monitoring
  RoleAssumptionAlarm:
    Type: AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-unauthorized-assume-role'
      AlarmDescription: >
        Alarm when the CI/CD cross-account role is assumed from
        an unexpected source or fails authentication.
      Namespace: CloudTrailMetrics
      MetricName: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-AssumeRoleFailures'
      Statistic: Sum
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 1
      Threshold: 3
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold
      TreatMissingData: notBreaching
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: Security Monitoring

Outputs:
  RoleArn:
    Description: ARN of the CI/CD cross-account role
    Value: !GetAtt CICDCrossAccountRoleClean.Arn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-RoleArn'

  RoleName:
    Description: Name of the CI/CD cross-account role
    Value: !Ref CICDCrossAccountRoleClean

  PolicyArn:
    Description: ARN of the CI/CD managed policy
    Value: !Ref CICDPolicy

  SecurityNotes:
    Description: Security implementation notes
    Value: >
      This role uses explicit account allowlists (not wildcards),
      requires ExternalId for confused deputy protection,
      enforces Organization membership via aws:PrincipalOrgID condition,
      requires secure transport, applies least-privilege permissions,
      and includes explicit deny on sensitive IAM operations.
      Wildcards in trust policies are intentionally avoided as they
      can allow unauthorized accounts to assume the role.
